title: Ashton Golding

Playing Career |url=http://www.rugbyleagueproject.org/players/ashton-golding/summary.html |access-date=28 February 2026 |website=Rugby League Project}} |updated = 23 May 2026 }} '''Ashton Golding''' (born 4 September 1996) is a {{Rlnt|Jamaica}} international [[rugby league]] footballer who plays as a [[Rugby league positions#Utility|utility]] for the [[Castleford Tigers]] in the [[Super League]]. Golding began his career with the [[Leeds Rhinos]], where he had progressed through the academy. He spent six years in the [[Super League]] with Leeds, during which time he also played for [[Featherstone Rovers]] in the [[RFL Championship|Championship]] on loan. Golding joined the [[Huddersfield Giants]] in 2020, where he would go on to make over 100 appearances and feature in the [[2022 Challenge Cup final]]. His stay also included a short loan spell at [[Bradford Bulls]] in the Championship. He signed for the Castleford Tigers in 2026. Described as a utility player,{{Cite web |date=23 October 2023 |title=Ashton Golding: Huddersfield Giants utility player signs new three-year deal with club |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/67192247 |access-date=1 March 2026 |website=BBC Sport}}{{Cite web |last=Craven |first=Dave |date=17 January 2024 |title=Ashton Golding ready to fix any job for Huddersfield Giants after goal change |url=https://www.mirror.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/huddersfield-giants-ashton-golding-rugby-31906551 |access-date=1 March 2026 |website=The Mirror}}{{Cite web |last=Walker |first=Callum |date=18 February 2026 |title=Castleford Tigers sign ex-Huddersfield Giants utility Ashton Golding |url=https://www.totalrl.com/castleford-tigers-sign-ex-huddersfield-giants-utility-ashton-golding/ |url-access=subscription |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20260221122634/https://www.totalrl.com/castleford-tigers-sign-ex-huddersfield-giants-utility-ashton-golding/ |archive-date=21 February 2026 |access-date=1 March 2026 |website=TotalRL}} Golding has played in several positions during his career, most recently being utilised primarily as a {{rlp|lf}} or {{rlp|hk}}. He has also operated as a {{rlp|fb|wg}}er or {{rlp|sr}}, as well as deputising at {{rlp|so}} and {{rlp|ce}}. ==Background== Golding was born in [[Bramley, Leeds]], West Yorkshire, England.{{Cite web |date=23 May 2017 |title=Leeds full-back Ashton Golding over the moon to sign new five-year contract |url=https://www.shropshirestar.com/sport/uk-sports/2017/05/23/leeds-full-back-ashton-golding-over-the-moon-to-sign-new-five-year-contract/ |url-status=dead |archive-url=http://web.archive.org/web/20231018201125/https://www.shropshirestar.com/sport/uk-sports/2017/05/23/leeds-full-back-ashton-golding-over-the-moon-to-sign-new-five-year-contract/ |archive-date=18 October 2023 |access-date=1 March 2026 |website=Shropshire Star}} He grew up in [[Stanningley]], West Yorkshire, and is of Jamaican descent.{{Cite web |date=20 September 2023 |title=Out Of Your League: Ash Golding |url=https://www.superleague.co.uk/article/3730/out-of-your-league-ash-golding |access-date=1 March 2026 |website=Super League}} He attended [[Priesthorpe School]] and studied [[sports science]] at [[Leeds Beckett University]].{{Cite web |date=13 August 2015 |title=Rising Rhinos stars head for university after exam success |url=https://www.yorkshireeveningpost.co.uk/education/rising-rhinos-stars-head-for-university-after-exam-success-1814454 |access-date=1 March 2026 |website=Yorkshire Evening Post}} Golding played junior rugby league for amateur club [[Stanningley SARLFC]] before joining the [[Leeds Rhinos]] academy system. In 2014, he was named under-19s player of the season.{{Cite web |date=22 December 2014 |title=Super League: Leeds Rhinos teenager Ashton Golding has agreed a new four-year deal |url=https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/12196/9617682/super-league-leeds-rhinos-teenager-ashton-golding-has-agreed-a-new-four-year-dea |access-date=1 March 2026 |website=Sky Sports}} ==Club career== ===Leeds Rhinos=== Golding made his [[Super League]] debut for Leeds in August 2014, brought on as an interchange against [[London Broncos]].{{Cite web |date=17 August 2014 |title=Super League: London Broncos 40-36 Leeds Rhinos |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/28764616 |access-date=1 March 2026 |website=BBC Sport}}{{Cite web |date=15 August 2016 |title=Leeds Rhinos Nostalgia: Leeds pay the price for fielding understrength side ahead of cup final |url=https://www.yorkshireeveningpost.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/leeds-rhinos-nostalgia-leeds-pay-the-price-for-fielding-understrength-side-ahead-of-cup-final-615928 |access-date=1 March 2026 |website=Yorkshire Evening Post}} In October 2014, he signed a four-year deal with Leeds. In March 2015, Golding made his first Super League start against [[Warrington Wolves|Warrington]] with first-choice fullback [[Zak Hardaker]] dropped.{{Cite web |date=16 March 2015 |title=Golding graduates at Rhinos with flying colours |url=https://www.yorkshirepost.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/golding-graduates-at-rhinos-with-flying-colours-1822436 |access-date=1 March 2026 |website=The Yorkshire Post}}{{Cite web |date=13 March 2015 |title=Zak Hardaker and Elliot Minchella of Leeds Rhinos face police probe |url=https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/12196/9757638/leeds-duo-zak-hardaker-and-elliot-minchella-face-police-probe |access-date=1 March 2026 |website=Sky Sports}} He was drafted into the team on three further occasions in the [[2015 Leeds Rhinos season|2015 season]].{{Cite web |date=4 September 2015 |title=Super League: Leeds Rhinos 18-32 St Helens |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/34115824 |access-date=1 March 2026 |website=BBC Sport}} Golding was included in [[Brian McDermott (rugby league)|Brian McDermott]]'s 25-man senior squad for [[2016 Leeds Rhinos season|2016]].{{Cite web |date=23 November 2015 |title=Leeds Rhinos hand squad numbers to teenagers Ash Handley, Jordan Lilley and Ashton Golding for 2016 |url=https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/12211/10076020/leeds-rhinos-hand-squad-numbers-to-teenagers-ash-handley-jordan-lilley-and-ashton-golding-for-2016 |access-date=1 March 2026 |website=Sky Sports}} He was understudy to the transfer-listed Hardaker at fullback, though registered most of his starts on the wing.{{Cite web |date=13 June 2016 |orig-date=9 June 2016 |title=Ashton Golding hopes 'mentor' Zak Hardaker is in no rush for exit door at Leeds Rhinos |url=https://www.yorkshirepost.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/ashton-golding-hopes-mentor-zak-hardaker-is-in-no-rush-for-exit-door-at-leeds-rhinos-1797309 |access-date=1 March 2026 |website=The Yorkshire Post}} He made thirteen appearances and scored three tries for Leeds, as well as playing on dual registration at [[Featherstone Rovers|Featherstone]] in the [[RFL Championship|Championship]]. At the start of the [[2017 Leeds Rhinos season|2017 season]], Golding was assigned Leeds' number 1 jersey.{{Cite web |date=28 February 2017 |orig-date=10 February 2017 |title=Leeds Rhinos: Ashton Golding in no mood to relinquish No 1 shirt |url=https://www.yorkshireeveningpost.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/leeds-rhinos-ashton-golding-in-no-mood-to-relinquish-no-1-shirt-604656 |access-date=1 March 2026 |website=Yorkshire Evening Post}} He earned praise for an outstanding defensive performance against [[St Helens R.F.C.|St Helens]] in round 1.{{Cite web |date=16 February 2017 |title=Ashton Golding says praise means little without Leeds win |url=https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/12211/10769842/ashton-golding-says-praise-means-little-without-leeds-win |access-date=1 March 2026 |website=Sky Sports}} He signed a new five-year contract in May having established himself as the Rhinos' first-choice fullback,{{Cite web |date=23 May 2017 |title=Ashton Golding: Leeds Rhinos full-back signs new five-year contract |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/40015583 |access-date=1 March 2026 |website=BBC Sport}} though by the end of the season he had been displaced by [[Jack Walker (rugby league)|Jack Walker]] and missed out on [[2017 Super League Grand Final|Grand Final]] selection.{{Cite web |date=11 September 2017 |orig-date=6 August 2017 |title=Leeds Rhinos star Ashton Golding delighted by Jack Walker's decision |url=https://www.yorkshirepost.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/leeds-rhinos-star-ashton-golding-delighted-by-jack-walkers-decision-843473 |access-date=1 March 2026 |website=The Yorkshire Post}}{{Cite web |date=19 January 2018 |title=Leeds Rhinos' Jack Walker adamant he was right to mull over future |url=https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/12211/11213716/leeds-rhinos-jack-walker-adamant-he-was-right-to-mull-over-future |access-date=1 March 2026 |website=Sky Sports}} ===Huddersfield Giants=== In 2020, Golding signed for [[Huddersfield Giants]]. On 26 May 2021, it was reported that he had signed for [[Bradford Bulls|Bradford]] in the [[RFL Championship]] on loan.{{cite web|website=Yorkshire Live|date=26 May 2021|accessdate=27 May 2021|title=Huddersfield Giants sextet set for Championship loan moves|url=https://www.examinerlive.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/huddersfield-giants-loan-championship-rugby-20683895.amp}} On 28 May 2022, Golding played for Huddersfield in their [[2022 Challenge Cup final]] loss to [[Wigan Warriors|Wigan]].{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.com/sport/rugby-league/61594770.amp/|title=Challenge Cup final: Huddersfield Giants 14-16 Wigan Warriors|website=www.bbc.co.uk|date=26 May 2022 }} Golding was limited to only eight matches with Huddersfield in the 2023 Super League season as the club finished ninth on the table and missed the playoffs.{{cite web|url=https://www.totalrl.com/ian-watson-explains-why-huddersfield-giants-underperformed-in-super-league-2023/#:~:text=HUDDERSFIELD%20GIANTS%20have%20been%20one,silverware%20once%20more%20this%20season./|title=Ian Watson explains why Huddersfield Giants underperformed in Super League 2023|website=www.totalrl.com|date=29 September 2023 }} Golding played 19 games for Huddersfield in the 2024 Super League season which saw the club finish 9th on the table.{{cite web|url=https://www.yorkshirepost.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/huddersfield-giants-34-castleford-tigers-10-hosts-win-dead-rubber-but-2024-season-one-to-forget-for-west-yorkshire-rivals-4789784/|title=Huddersfield Giants 34 Castleford Tigers 10: Hosts win dead rubber but 2024 season one to forget for West Yorkshire rivals|website=www.yorkshirepost.co.uk}} Golding played 27 games for Huddersfield in the 2025 Super League season as the club finished 10th on the table.{{cite web|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/huddersfield-giants-coach-sounds-nrl-warning-as-honest-admission-made-over-2025-shortcomings/|title=Huddersfield Giants coach sounds NRL warning as honest admission made over 2025 shortcomings|website=www.loverugbyleague.com}} On 16 November 2025, Golding departed Huddersfield by mutual consent.{{cite web|website=Total RL|date=16 Nov 2025|author=Doug Thomson|title=Ashton Golding parts ways with Huddersfield Giants|url=https://www.totalrl.com/ashton-golding-parts-ways-with-huddersfield-giants/}} ===Castleford Tigers=== On 18 February 2026, [[Castleford Tigers]] announced the signing of Golding on a deal until the end of the [[2026 Castleford Tigers season|2026 season]].{{Cite web |date=18 February 2026 |title=Castleford Tigers sign full-back Golding |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/articles/c99jpyx3dg2o |access-date=1 March 2026 |website=BBC Sport}} Though his signing was announced shortly following a long-term injury to fullback [[Blake Taaffe]], Tigers head coach [[Ryan Carr (rugby league)|Ryan Carr]] – whom Golding had previously played under at Featherstone – confirmed he was brought in to cover several positions across the forwards and backs.{{Cite web |last=Smith |first=Peter |date=19 February 2026 |title=Castleford Tigers boss reveals full-back plan after Blake Taaffe injury as Ashton Golding signing explained |url=https://www.yorkshireeveningpost.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/castleford-tigers-boss-reveals-full-back-plan-after-blake-taaffe-injury-as-ashton-golding-signing-explained-5604523 |access-date=1 March 2026 |website=Yorkshire Evening Post}}{{Cite web |last=Olawumi |first=Ben |date=19 February 2026 |title=Castleford Tigers coach clarifies Ashton Golding signing as fitness update issued |url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/castleford-tigers-coach-clarifies-ashton-golding-signing |access-date=1 March 2026 |website=Love Rugby League}} He made his debut in round 3 against former team Huddersfield, crossing for a late try to seal victory.{{Cite web |last=Aloia |first=Andrew |date=27 February 2026 |title=Castleford beat Giants for first Super League win |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/articles/cx2drzj29nno |access-date=1 March 2026 |website=BBC Sport}}{{Cite web |last=Cheshire |first=Adam |date=28 February 2026 |title=Photos and reaction: Tigers have 'smile on their faces' in first Super League win of season over Giants |url=https://www.wakefieldexpress.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/photos-and-reaction-tigers-have-smile-on-their-faces-in-first-super-league-win-of-season-over-giants-5615288 |access-date=1 March 2026 |website=Wakefield Express}} ==International career== Golding played for {{Rlnt|England}} Academy in 2014 and was named man-of-the-match in their win against the [[Australian Schoolboys rugby league team|Australian Schoolboys]]. On 12 October 2017, Golding was named in {{Rlnt|Jamaica}}'s squad to take on {{Rlnt|France}} in a [[2017 Rugby League World Cup|World Cup]] warm-up game.{{cite web|url=http://www.yorkshireeveningpost.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/leeds-rhinos/leeds-rhinos-keinhorst-back-with-germany-after-five-year-absence-1-8801841 |title=Leeds Rhinos Keinhorst back with Germany after five year absence |publisher=Yorkshire Evening Post |date=12 October 2017 |accessdate=12 October 2017}} He scored a try on debut as Jamaica fell to a 34–12 defeat at [[Stade Gilbert Brutus]].{{Cite web |date=October 2017 |title=France beat Jamaica |url=http://www.catalansdragons.com/en/articles-9/159-5407-france-beat-jamaica/ |access-date=1 March 2026 |website=Catalans Dragons}}{{cite web|last=Daly|first=Phil|date=16 October 2017|title=Golding marks Jamaica début with a try|url=https://www.therhinos.co.uk/2017/10/16/golding-marks-jamaica-debut-try|accessdate=6 March 2018|website=Leeds Rhinos|url-status=dead}} Golding was one of two Super League players to be selected in Jamaica's 29-man squad for the [[2018 Americas Rugby League Championship]] held at [[Hodges Stadium]] in [[Jacksonville, Florida]].{{Cite web |last=Darbyshire |first=Drew |date=17 October 2018 |title=Ben Jones-Bishop and Ashton Golding named in Jamaica squad for Americas Championship |url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/ben-jones-bishop-and-ashton-golding-named-in-jamaica-squad-for-americas-championship |access-date=1 March 2026 |website=Love Rugby League}} He scored a try in their semi-final win against {{Rlnt|Canada}} and was named man-of-the-match in their 10–16 victory over the {{Rlnt|United States}} in the final.{{Cite web |date=16 November 2018 |title=USA to face Jamaica in World Cup Qualification Play off |url=http://www.rlif.com/article/8533/ |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20190517054850/http://www.rlif.com/article/8533/ |archive-date=17 May 2019 |access-date=1 March 2026 |website=Rugby League International Federation}}{{Cite web |last=Smith |first=Peter |date=14 October 2022 |title=Jamaica v Ireland: We'll bring new fans to rugby league World Cup says ex-Leeds Rhinos ace Ashton Golding |url=https://www.yorkshireeveningpost.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/jamaica-v-ireland-well-bring-new-fans-to-rugby-league-world-cup-says-ex-leeds-rhinos-ace-ashton-golding-3879177 |access-date=1 March 2026 |website=Yorkshire Evening Post}} This marked Jamaica's first ever win against the USA and secured qualification to the 2021 Rugby League World Cup.{{Cite web |date=18 November 2018 |title=Jamaica secure place at 2021 Rugby League World Cup |url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/jamaica-secure-place-at-2021-rugby-league-world-cup |access-date=1 March 2026 |website=Love Rugby League}} In October 2019, he featured at fullback for Jamaica against the [[England Knights]] at [[Headingley Rugby Stadium]].{{Cite web |last=Smith |first=Peter |date=20 October 2019 |title=England Knights 38 Jamaica 6: Minikin hat-trick scures Knights win |url=https://www.yorkshireeveningpost.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/england-knights-38-jamaica-6-minikin-hat-trick-scures-knights-win-817159 |access-date=1 March 2026 |website=Yorkshire Evening Post}} On 15 October 2021, Golding played for Jamaica against the England Knights at [[Wheldon Road]].{{Cite web |date=15 October 2021 |title=Youthful England Knights defeat Jamaica |url=https://www.rugby-league.com/article/36158/youthful-england-knights-defeat-jamaica |access-date=1 March 2026 |website=Rugby Football League}} The following week, he played in Jamaica's 30–30 draw with {{Rlnt|Scotland}} at [[Post Office Road]].{{Cite web |date=24 October 2021 |title=Jamaica fightback to draw with Scotland |url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/jamaica-fightback-to-draw-with-scotland |access-date=1 March 2026 |website=Love Rugby League}} In October 2022, Golding was appointed captain of the Jamaica national team ahead of the [[2021 Men's Rugby League World Cup|2021 World Cup]], their maiden tournament.{{Cite web |last=Staniforth |first=Mark |date=12 October 2022 |title='We're not just a gimmick': Ashton Golding out to prove Jamaica are serious about rugby league |url=https://www.yorkshirepost.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/were-not-just-a-gimmick-ashton-golding-out-to-prove-jamaica-are-serious-about-rugby-league-3877408 |access-date=1 March 2026 |website=The Yorkshire Post}} He played in a warm-up match against [[Cumbria rugby league team|Cumbria]].{{Cite web |date=8 October 2022 |title=Jamaica suffered defeat in Cumbria in World Cup warm-up |url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/jamaica-suffered-defeat-in-cumbria-in-world-cup-warm-up |access-date=1 March 2026 |website=Love Rugby League}} He was assigned the number 13 shirt and played in all three matches of the campaign, against {{Rlnt|Ireland}}, {{Rlnt|New Zealand}} and {{Rlnt|Lebanon}}, as Jamaica exited at the group stage.{{Cite web |date=22 October 2022 |title=Ashton Golding joyous over Jamaica's first-ever World Cup try |url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/ashton-golding-joyous-over-jamaicas-first-ever-world-cup-try |access-date=1 March 2026 |website=Love Rugby League}} On 25 October 2025, Golding captained Jamaica in their [[2026 Men's Rugby League World Cup qualification|2026 World Cup qualifying]] play-off against {{Rlnt|France}}.{{Cite web |last=Bower |first=Aaron |date=22 October 2025 |title=Super League quintet get Jamaica call for World Cup qualifier as Wigan man set for debut |url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/super-league-quintet-get-jamaica-call-for-world-cup-qualifier-as-wigan-man-set-for-debut |access-date=1 March 2026 |website=Love Rugby League}} They were defeated 36–0 at [[Stadium Municipal d'Albi|Stade Municipal d'Albi]] and were unable to secure a place at a second consecutive World Cup.{{Cite web |last=Bird |first=Peter |date=25 October 2025 |title=France see off Jamaica to reach World Cup |url=https://www.totalrl.com/france-see-off-jamaica-to-reach-world-cup/ |url-access=subscription |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20251222020504/https://www.totalrl.com/france-see-off-jamaica-to-reach-world-cup/ |archive-date=22 December 2025 |access-date=1 March 2026 |website=TotalRL}}{{Cite web |date=25 October 2025 |title=France 36–0 Jamaica |url=https://europeanrugbyleague.com/fixtures/1386 |access-date=1 March 2026 |website=European Rugby League}} ==
